description: Comprehensive gzip decompression test corpus for rapidgzip-rs. Covers synthetic edge cases, classic compression benchmarks (Canterbury, Silesia), large real-world data, and domain-specific formats to thoroughly exercise a parallel gzip decoder.

datasets.name: Empty file
datasets.notes: Zero-length input produces the minimal valid gzip stream (20 bytes: 10-byte header + empty DEFLATE block + 4-byte CRC32 + 4-byte ISIZE). Critical for verifying the decoder handles empty streams without crash or spurious output.

datasets.name: Single byte 'A'
datasets.notes: Smallest non-empty payload. Verifies single-byte output with no off-by-one errors in buffer management or CRC computation.

datasets.name: 1 MiB of null bytes
datasets.notes: Maximally compressible data. Produces very long back-references in DEFLATE, achieving ~1000:1 compression ratio. Stress-tests distance/length decoding and the sliding window at extreme lengths.

datasets.name: 1 MiB of random data
datasets.notes: Incompressible random data. The compressed output will be larger than the input. Exercises literal encoding paths and verifies correct handling of data that cannot be compressed via back-references.

datasets.name: Repeating 8-byte pattern (1 MiB)
datasets.notes: Short pattern repeated densely. Creates short-distance back-reference chains that stress the sliding window and length/distance decoding differently from long runs of zeros.

datasets.name: Concatenated gzip members
datasets.notes: Two gzip members concatenated (Hello + World = HelloWorld). Tests that the decoder correctly detects member boundaries, resets internal state between members, and concatenates decompressed output in order.

datasets.name: 1 MiB of ASCII spaces
datasets.notes: All 0x20 bytes. Similar to zeros but exercises a different literal value. Ensures the decoder handles all byte values in the output, not just null.

datasets.name: 1 MiB of 0xFF bytes
datasets.notes: All 0xFF bytes. Tests the highest byte value to ensure no sign-extension bugs or issues with byte values >= 0x80 in the decompressed output.

datasets.name: 10 MiB cycling bytes, gzip -1
datasets.notes: 10 MiB of cycling byte values 0-255 compressed with fastest level. Produces different DEFLATE structure (simpler Huffman trees, larger blocks) than max compression. Pairs with synth-level9 to verify bit-exact decompressed output regardless of compression level.

datasets.name: 10 MiB cycling bytes, gzip -9
datasets.notes: Same source data as synth-level1 but compressed with maximum level. Exercises complex dynamic Huffman trees, long back-references, and smaller blocks. Decompressed output must be bit-exact identical to synth-level1.

datasets.name: Alice in Wonderland
datasets.notes: Classic English literary text (~150KB) with natural language patterns. Good representative of text decompression workloads. Tests medium-length back-references typical of natural language.

datasets.name: As You Like It (Shakespeare)
datasets.notes: Shakespearean play. Different vocabulary and line structure from prose. Tests archaic English with dialogue formatting and stage directions.

datasets.name: HTML source
datasets.notes: HTML with mixed tags and text. Tests decompression of structured markup where tag names create repeating patterns compressible via back-references.

datasets.name: C source code (fields.c)
datasets.notes: Small C source file (~11KB). Tests decompression of programming language text with keywords, operators, and short identifiers.

datasets.name: LISP source (grammar.lsp)
datasets.notes: Very small file (~3.7KB). Tests that the decoder handles tiny inputs correctly, including cases where the entire file is a single DEFLATE block.

datasets.name: Excel spreadsheet (kennedy.xls)
datasets.notes: Excel spreadsheet (~1MB). Binary format with structured data. Tests decompression of non-text binary content with record structures and varied entropy.

datasets.name: Technical writing (lcet10.txt)
datasets.notes: Technical writing (~427KB). Specialized vocabulary with repeated domain-specific terms. Tests back-references capturing repeated jargon.

datasets.name: Plato's Republic (poetry)
datasets.notes: Poetry (~482KB). Line-oriented with shorter lines than prose. Tests decompression of text with frequent line breaks and rhythmic patterns.

datasets.name: CCITT fax image (ptt5)
datasets.notes: CCITT fax test image (~513KB). Binary image data with runs of same-value pixels. Tests decompression of binary data that has its own run-length structure, creating interesting interactions with DEFLATE back-references.

datasets.name: SPARC executable (sum)
datasets.notes: SPARC executable (~38KB). Binary file with ELF headers, code sections, and data. Tests mixed binary content with varying entropy (high in code, low in padding).

datasets.name: GNU manual page (xargs.1)
datasets.notes: Very small man page (~4.2KB). Tests the decoder's handling of small inputs where gzip wrapper overhead is significant relative to payload.

datasets.name: E. coli genome
datasets.notes: Complete E. coli genome (~4.6MB). Only 4-character alphabet (A/C/G/T) with biological repetition patterns. Very different from English text - tests decompression of data with a tiny alphabet and bio repeat structures.

datasets.name: King James Bible
datasets.notes: King James Bible (~4MB). Very large text with archaic English, verse numbers, and repetitive book/chapter/verse structure. Tests long-range text patterns.

datasets.name: CIA World Factbook
datasets.notes: CIA World Factbook (~2.5MB). Structured text with country entries following templates. Highly repetitive structure tests medium-distance back-references.

datasets.name: Dickens collected works
datasets.notes: 14 Dickens works concatenated (~10MB). Large English text. Big enough to exercise parallel chunk splitting. Tests natural language text decompression at scale.

datasets.name: Mozilla 1.0 tarred executables
datasets.notes: Mozilla 1.0 tarred directory (~51MB). Contains executables, JAR archives, HTML, XML, text - extremely diverse content in one file. Tests decoder's ability to handle rapid entropy changes across parallel chunks. Large enough to thoroughly exercise parallel decompression.

datasets.name: Medical MRI image
datasets.notes: DICOM MRI scan of a head (~10MB). 19 planes of medical imaging data. Binary structured data with specific voxel patterns.

datasets.name: NCI chemical database
datasets.notes: Chemical structure database in SDF format (~34MB). Molecular structures, coordinates, and properties. Highly compressible structured text with repeated field labels. Tests database-style records with strong inter-record patterns.

datasets.name: OpenOffice DLL
datasets.notes: OpenOffice DLL (~6MB). Windows PE binary with code, imports, and data sections. Tests a single binary executable with varied entropy.

datasets.name: Open Source Database Benchmark
datasets.notes: MySQL database file from OSDB benchmark (~10MB). Binary database format with mixed record types. Tests structured binary data typical of database workloads.

datasets.name: Chlopi by Reymont (Polish text)
datasets.notes: Polish novel 'Chlopi' as uncompressed PDF (~6.6MB). Contains non-ASCII characters (Polish diacritics in PDF encoding). Essential for testing that the decoder handles multi-byte and extended Latin characters correctly.

datasets.name: Samba 2.2 source code
datasets.notes: Tarred Samba 2.2 source code (~22MB). C source, headers, docs, Makefiles, scripts. Large enough for parallel decompression. Tests mixed code/text typical of software distribution.

datasets.name: SAO star catalog
datasets.notes: SAO star catalog with 258,996 stars (~7.3MB). Binary fixed-length records. Tests structured binary data with astronomical coordinates.

datasets.name: Webster's 1913 Dictionary (HTML)
datasets.notes: 1913 Webster Dictionary in HTML (~41MB). Very large HTML/text hybrid with repetitive markup around word definitions. Large enough to thoroughly exercise parallel chunking.

datasets.name: XML document collection
datasets.notes: Concatenation of 21 XML files (~5.3MB). Structured markup with repeated tags and mixed content. Tests decompression where tag names provide excellent back-reference targets while content varies.

datasets.name: X-ray medical image
datasets.notes: 12-bit grayscale X-ray of a child's hand (~8.5MB). Medical imaging data with spatial correlations. Tests binary image data where pixel neighborhoods create specific compression patterns.

datasets.name: Wikipedia XML dump (first 100MB)
datasets.notes: First 100MB of English Wikipedia XML dump from 2006. UTF-8 encoded XML with article titles, revision metadata, and wikitext. Very large text with mixed content. Excellent parallel decompression test due to size and heterogeneous content.

datasets.name: Illumina FASTQ (ERR2432917)
datasets.notes: Real Illumina sequencing data from NCBI SRA. FASTQ format with quality scores. Already gzip-compressed by bioinformatics tools. Tests decompression of a real gzipped file from the wild. File must be present at project root.

datasets.name: Linux 6.6 kernel source tarball
datasets.notes: Linux 6.6 LTS kernel source tarball (~200MB compressed, ~1.3GB decompressed tar). The ultimate parallel decompression stress test: huge, with extremely varied content (C source, headers, Kconfig, Makefiles, device trees, scripts). Large enough to saturate many parallel decode threads.

datasets.name: Complete Works of Shakespeare (Gutenberg)
datasets.notes: Complete Works of Shakespeare from Project Gutenberg (~5.6MB). Large text combining plays, sonnets, and narrative poems. Complements Canterbury's As You Like It with the full Shakespeare canon.
